title: import id_from_timestamp can overflow u16 seq counter
description: import.rs id_from_timestamp: the seq variable is u16 (max 65535) but the function unconditionally increments it when published_ms == last_ms with no overflow check. If a Mastodon archive contains more than 65535 posts in the same millisecond (unlikely in real data, but possible in bulk-imported/mass-generated archives), seq wraps to 0 causing duplicate IDs which will fail the UNIQUE constraint on posts.ap_id (since the ID is embedded in the ap_id). This is a latent correctness issue — the runtime behavior is an opaque INSERT failure logged as 'Skipping post'. The real-time generate_id() correctly handles this by spinning. Fix: add wrapping check or use a wider type.
